ha
harsh.haripara
Jacob Beaudry , We already have a refresh token API that runs every 4 hours. As long as the system is on and the website remains open in the browser, the user can continue using the platform without logging in again, since the API will return a valid response. However, if the browser tab is closed or the system is shut down, the refresh token API cannot run, which will cause the user’s session to expire and require them to log in again.

Since you mentioned that some users in your office tend to keep their systems running, we’ve implemented an additional feature where the system will automatically log out all users at 12 AM midnight Eastern Time. This is currently available in the Dev environment for testing and will be moved to UAT after testing is complete. Thank you for your suggestion.

harsh.haripara The session timer would be important anyways because even if the user isn't logged in for 30 days from the creation of the token, even if they logged in the day before the token had expired and continued to be logged in for another few days they would be defeating the point of the 2FA by bypassing the expiry of the token.

Jacob Beaudry
harsh.haripara During testing I noticed that the 30 minute timer works successfully in that a user would log out and then upon login they would be prompted to enter in the OTP.

However we do not have a company policy that forces a user to log out and back in at any specific time interval, which could potentially see someone be logged in for more than 30 days. Could a session timer be implemented in such a way that at 12 AM (midnight) Eastern Time on that day the token expires, their session is terminated and the employee must log back in when they come into the office for the day?

harsh.haripara
Joshua Smaltz

Note: There's a functionality in this where, once a user does Two factor authentication (TFA), they will not be asked to do TFA again for 30 days. After 30 days, they'll be asked to do TFA again and to test it we've reduced that time from 30 days to 30mins. So 30 mins after TFA is done, you'll need to logout and login and you'll be asked to do TFA again.

19/09/2024 10:08 PM

ha
harsh.haripara
Joshua Smaltz 

We have done the feasibility analysis of this requirement. Flow for TFA will be as mentioned below:

We'll be doing TFA using email for now as if we want to do TFA using mobile number then we'll have to use third party services which will increase the cost as well as the complexity of this task.
Once the user logs in with valid credentials, they'll be able to see a screen to enter the OTP (One time passcode) they received on their email ID.
OTP sent to the user will be expired after 10 mins.
User will be able to request for another OTP using "Resend" button once every 1 min.
TFA will be mandatory for all users.
Once the TFA is done, the system will not ask the user to do TFA for 30 days.
When we deploy this feature, if any user is currently using the website then they'll see a validation asking them to logout & log in again.

We estimate that it'll require around 80 hours of work:
45 hours - Development
35 hours - Testing including testing on Dev, UAT & Prod environment.
Let us know your thoughts on this, and then we can move forward with this requirement.

CC: Shruti Nautiyal
